message:
fix: expand Docker prerequisites and fix verified script execution

1. Expand container prerequisite packages to match what ACFS install.sh
   pre-installs: git, unzip, xz-utils, tar, jq, build-essential, sudo,
   gnupg, libssl-dev, pkg-config, zsh. Fixes bun (needed unzip),
   ohmyzsh (needed git), dcg (needed xz-utils), br (needed jq).

2. Change verified install script execution from `bash 'file'` to
   `bash -s -- < 'file'` — matches the curl|bash pipe behavior and
   avoids issues with installers that check $0 or need stdin semantics.

3. Remove tmpfs mount for /tmp entirely — Docker's overlay filesystem
   allows exec by default, and Bollard's tmpfs option handling was
   adding noexec regardless of our "exec" flag.

Test results: 8/10 pass (up from 5/10). Remaining 2 failures are
legitimate ACFS installer bugs (dcg exits 1 on success, rustup needs -y).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
